title: LGBTQ2+ Helplines - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Crisis Hotlines > LGBTQ2+ Helplines 3 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter LGBTQ2+ Youth Older Adults Transgender Individuals Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF LESBIAN, GAY, BISEXUAL AND TRANSGENDER NATIONAL HELP CENTER LGBT NATIONAL SENIOR HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a hotline for gay, lesbian, bisexual, and transgender older adults to talk with trained peer-counselor volunteers about issues including isolation, relationship concerns, bullying, HIV/AIDS, etc. 2261 Market Street #296 San Francisco, CA 94114 Eligibility: Age 50 and older who identify as gay, lesbian, bisexual, and transgender Hours: Monday-Friday 11am-8pm, Saturday 9am-2pm Email (888) 234-7243 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LESBIAN, GAY, BISEXUAL AND TRANSGENDER NATIONAL HELP CENTER LGBT NATIONAL YOUTH TALKLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides telephone, online private one-to-one chat, and email peer support for LGBT teens and young adults to talk about coming-out issues, relationship concerns, parent issues, school problems, HIV/AIDS anxiety, safer-sex information, etc. 2261 Market Street #296 San Francisco, CA 94114 Eligibility: Ages 13-25 who identify as gay, lesbian, bisexual, and transgender Hours: Monday-Friday 11am-8pm, Saturday 9am-2pm Email (800) 246-7743 800-246 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LESBIAN, GAY, BISEXUAL AND TRANSGENDER NATIONAL HELP CENTER LGBT NATIONAL HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides telephone, online private one-to-one chat, and email peer support for LGBT callers of all ages to talk about coming-out issues, relationship concerns, bullying, workplace issues, HIV/AIDS anxiety, safer-sex information, etc. Also maintains the GLBT National Resource Database, an online resource database with information on youth groups, social and support organizations, and gay-friendly religious organizations, sports leagues, student groups, etc. 2261 Market Street #296 San Francisco, CA 94114 Eligibility: Individuals who identify as gay, lesbian, bisexual, and transgender Hours: Monday-Friday 11am-8pm, Saturday 9am-2pm Email (888) 843-4564 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE TREVOR PROJECT TREVOR PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides crisis intervention, suicide prevention services, peer support, and outside referrals relating to sexual orientation or gender identity issues. Eligibility: Ages 13-24 who identify as lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, or questioning Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (866) 488-7386 24 7 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANS LIFELINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a volunteer-run hotline that offers crisis support and connection to resources where possible such as passport and state identification help to reflect gender identity. While the goal is to prevent self-harm, it is also a warmline where people can call with a variety of needs (i.e., how to access transition-related health care, where to find local support groups). The hotline is staffed by trans-identifying individuals. Operators will only call emergency services with the caller's consent. 548 Market Street Pmb 15337 San Francisco, CA 941045401 Eligibility: Any trans-identifying person and anyone who may be questioning their gender identity Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-6pm; closed some holidays Email (877) 565-8860 Get Directions Visit Website More Details No Mapping Information Available
